description:
The Devil's AI Dictionary cuts through the hype with sharp, plain-English definitions of AI terms, product labels, and buzzwords. Every entry includes a devil's definition (the irreverent take), a plain definition (the real explanation), why the term exists, how it gets misused, and what it actually means in practice.

Browse A-Z or by category. Search across 100+ entries covering machine learning, large language models, agents, embeddings, and the rest of the AI lexicon. Four reading themes - Book, Codex, Absolutely, and Night - let you pick your preferred look.

No accounts. No tracking. No ads. Just a dictionary that tells you what AI words actually mean, and why you should be sceptical when someone uses them.
keywords: ai dictionary,artificial intelligence,machine learning,llm,gpt,ai glossary,deep learning,ai hype
whatsNew:
The glossary is broader, the browsing flow is cleaner, and the app is less fussy about keeping itself up to date.

Version 1.0.2 adds Categories, folds Browse and Search into one clearer surface, refreshes the Home and Settings screens, and brings in a larger run of new entries, including recent AI evasions and figures such as Sundar, Karpathy, Levels, and bolt.new.

You can now pull to refresh from Home, move through categories with fewer awkward taps, and rely on sturdier catalogue updates and notification delivery. We also added an animated splash screen and tidied a clutch of mobile rough edges so the dictionary feels faster, clearer, and more deliberate.
